name: Saxony-Anhalt (Germany)

colors.symbolism: from the historic colours of Anhalt and Prussian Saxony
from the historic colours of Anhalt and Prussian Saxony

description: Two equal horizontal stripes of yellow over black with the state coat of arms charged on the centre.
history: The yellow-and-black bicolour was adopted in 1991 as the civil flag of the re-established Land of Saxony-Anhalt after German reunification, drawing on the historic colours of Anhalt and Prussian Saxony. The state flag bears the coat of arms in the centre. A revised version of the design was promulgated in 2017.

features.symbolism: Yellow and black combine the historic colours of the Duchy of Anhalt and the Prussian Province of Saxony, the two main components of the modern Land.

The shield combines the black-and-gold barry with green crancelin (rue crown) of the Duchy of Saxony, the Brandenburg eagle, and the crowned Ascanian bear of Anhalt; together they represent the principal historic territories united in modern Saxony-Anhalt.
